JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE SET FOR JUL-08-2009 CONTINUED TO JUL-08-2009 AT 1:30 PM IN 602
ORDER (1) EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS TO COREAS' DECLARATION AND (2) PLAINTIFF'S STATEMENT OF UNDISPUTED FACTS

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR SUMM. JUDG. IS GRANTED. DEFT'S OBJECTIONS TO PLTF'S DECLARATION SUSTAINED. THE COURT DOES NOT RULE ON DISPUTED FACTS. PLTF'S OBJECTIONS TO VALDEZ DECLARATION SUSTAINED, AND OBJECTION TO KHUU EXH. "A" SUSTAINED, ALL OTHER OBJECS OVERRULED. AS TO THE 1ST AND 3RD CAUSES OF ACTION FOR SEXUAL HARASSMENT/HOSTILE WORKING ENVIRONMENT, CHAN'S BEHAVIOR ALTHOUGH RUDE, INAPPROPRIATE, AND OFFENSIVE, WAS NOT SEVERE AND PERVASIVE AS DEFINED BY CASE LAW. (SEE MOKLER V. COUNTY OF ORANGE (2007) 157 CAL.APP. 4TH 121, DEFTS UMF #3.) NO TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT CREATED THAT AS SOON AS THE MTA BECAME AWARE OF CHEN'S INAPPROPRIATE SEXUAL CONDUCT, IT TOOK IMMEDIATE REMEDIAL ACTION. (DEFTS UMF 3-4.) IT IS UNDISPUTED THAT PLTF DID NOT COMPLAIN OF CHEN'S BEHAVIOR UNTIL DECEMBER 2006 AND THE CITY PROCEEDED TO CHANGE CHEN'S SHIFT AND EVENTUALLY TERMINATED HIM AS SOON AS THEY VERIFIED THE NATURE OF HIS BEHAVIOR. AS TO THE 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION, FOR DISCRIMINATION, NO TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT CREATED THAT PLAINTIFF SUFFERED AN ADVERSE EMPLOYMENT ACTION. AS TO THE 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR RETALIATION, NO TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT RAISED: PLTF. SUFFERED NO ADVERSE EMPLOYMENT ACTION AND THERE IS NO EVIDENCE THAT HER CONDITIONS OF EMPLOYMENT WERE MATERIALLY AFFECTED. AS TO THE 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FAILURE TO PREVENT, THERE ARE NO TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT REGARDING HARASSMENT AND RETALIATION. THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS IS CONCEDED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
ORDER GRANTING DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jun-30-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Appearances: H. Larry Elam, III, The Venardi Elam Firm, LLP, (925) 937-3903, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Margaret W. Baumgartner, Dep. City Atty, CCSF, (415) 554-3859, appearing for Deft & moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for summ. judg. is granted. Deft's objections to pltf's declaration sustained. The court does not rule on disputed facts. Pltf's objections to Valdez declaration sustained, and objection to Khuu Exh. "A" sustained, all other objecs overruled. As to the 1st and 3rd causes of action for sexual harassment/hostile working environment, Chan's behavior although rude, inappropriate, and offensive, was not severe and pervasive as defined by case law. (See Mokler v. County of Orange (2007) 157 Cal.App. 4th 121, Defts UMF #3.) No triable issue of fact created that as soon as the MTA became aware of Chen's inappropriate sexual conduct, it took immediate remedial action. (Defts UMF 3-4.) It is undisputed that pltf did not complain of Chen's behavior until December 2006 and the City proceeded to change Chen's shift and eventually terminated him as soon as they verified the nature of his behavior. As to the 2nd cause of action, for Discrimination, no triable issue of fact created that plaintiff suffered an adverse employment action. As to the 4th cause of action for retaliation, no triable issue of fact raised: pltf. suffered no adverse employment action and there is no evidence that her conditions of employment were materially affected. As to the 6th cause of action for Failure to prevent, there are no triable issues of fact regarding harassment and retaliation. The fifth cause of action for Intentional Infliction of emotional distress is conceded. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jun-25-2009 Continued To Jun-30-09 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
